agent.name: performance-cost-auditor
agent.role: Find N+1 queries, missing indexes, redundant API calls, oversized bundles, unnecessary re-renders, unoptimized images, and third-party cost risks.

findings.title: Unoptimized SELECT * Queries
findings.description: Multiple Supabase queries use .select('*') to fetch all columns. This wastes bandwidth and memory, especially for tables with large text or JSONB fields like projects and task_prompts.

proof_hooks.summary: SELECT * used instead of explicit column list

findings.impact: Unnecessary bandwidth and memory usage on every query; degrades performance for large JSONB columns in projects and task_prompts tables.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace .select('*') with an explicit column list containing only the fields consumed by callers. Start with projects and task_prompts adapters.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Removing columns from SELECT may break callers that reference fields not included in the new list. Audit all usage sites before removing columns.
tests_needed: Unit: verify each adapter only returns the explicitly selected columns
Integration: confirm no runtime TypeErrors from missing fields after narrowing SELECT

history.notes: Discovered during static analysis of Supabase query patterns.

findings.title: Missing staleTime in React Query Hooks
findings.description: Almost all project hooks (useProjects, useProjectTasks, etc.) omit the staleTime configuration. With the default of 0ms, this triggers redundant network requests on every window focus, component remount, and network reconnect.

proof_hooks.actual: Missing staleTime property in useQuery config object

findings.impact: Redundant Supabase requests fire on every window focus, component remount, and network reconnect, increasing DB read load and degrading perceived performance.

suggested_fix.approach: Add staleTime (e.g. 5 * 60 * 1000 for 5 minutes) to all useQuery hooks for data that does not change frequently. Use a shared queryOptions helper to keep it DRY.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Setting staleTime too high risks showing stale data after mutations. Pair with appropriate invalidation calls in mutation hooks.
tests_needed: Unit: confirm hooks include staleTime in their query options
Integration: verify data does not refetch unnecessarily on window focus after mutation invalidation

history.notes: Identified via code inspection of all useQuery call sites in hooks.ts.

findings.title: Missing Code Splitting for Large Libraries
findings.description: The application relies on heavy dependencies (monaco-editor, @xyflow/react, html2canvas, jspdf, recharts) but lacks any React.lazy() or dynamic import() code-splitting boundaries. This forces all users to download the full weight of these libraries upfront, severely impacting initial load performance.

proof_hooks.summary: No React.lazy() or dynamic import() found in App.tsx
Bundle stats confirm large deps compiled into main chunk
No dynamic imports in source; all heavy deps in main bundle
proof_hooks.expected: Dynamic chunks for editor and flow diagram components
proof_hooks.actual: No dynamic imports found in src; large dependencies statically compiled to main bundle

findings.impact: All users download the full weight of monaco-editor, @xyflow/react, html2canvas, jspdf, and recharts on first load, regardless of which route they visit. Significantly increases Time-to-Interactive.

suggested_fix.approach: Wrap heavy route-specific components (IDE/Editor, Flow diagram views) in React.lazy() and Suspense boundaries. Add Vite manualChunks for monaco-editor and @xyflow/react to control chunk naming and caching.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Lazy-loading can cause layout shifts during Suspense fallback. Ensure meaningful loading states are provided. Test that all lazy routes load correctly in production builds.
tests_needed: Build: verify heavy deps are split into separate chunks in dist/
E2E: confirm lazy-loaded routes render correctly after navigation

history.notes: Identified via bundle stats analysis and absence of dynamic imports in App.tsx.

next_actions.action: Audit SELECT * queries in adapter files and replace with specific column whitelists.
next_actions.rationale: Reduces unnecessary data transfer and memory use on every query fetch.

next_actions.action: Add staleTime configuration to all React Query useQuery hooks.
next_actions.rationale: Eliminates redundant Supabase requests triggered on window focus and remounts.

next_actions.action: Implement React.lazy() for route-level code splitting, especially for the IDE/Editor and Flow routes.
next_actions.rationale: Reduces initial bundle size and improves Time-to-Interactive for users who never visit heavy routes.
